skill.name: blocklet-updater
skill.description: Automates Blocklet release preparation by bumping the version, building the project when needed, validating metadata, and creating a release bundle.
skill.summary: Bump, validate, build, and bundle a Blocklet project release.
skill.author: ArcBlock

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives from markdown prose, inline code, and fenced command examples. The skill has a real medium-risk behavior because it instructs the agent to run blocklet and pnpm commands, including dependency installation and project build scripts, inside the user workspace.

medium_findings.title: Project-Controlled Commands During Release Workflow
medium_findings.description: The workflow directs the agent to run blocklet version, pnpm install, pnpm run build, blocklet meta, and blocklet bundle commands. This is legitimate release automation, but pnpm install and build scripts can execute code from the current project and its dependencies, so users should apply it only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command instructions are explicit and match the skill purpose. The risk depends on the target project contents, so the behavior is elevated but not malicious by itself.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography indicators, but the referenced files are markdown documentation, front matter, inline command names, and fenced shell examples. No Ruby code, cryptographic operation, obfuscation, or malware intent was found in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain markdown text and shell command examples rather than executable Ruby or crypto code. The file set contains only documentation files for the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution Required by Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires command execution for version bumping, dependency installation, project builds, metadata validation, and bundle creation. These commands can modify files and run project-defined scripts, so marketplace users need a trust warning.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly lists shell commands that an agent should execute. The commands are expected for the skill, but they are still sensitive operations.

content.value_statement: Blocklet release steps can fail when versioning, build output, or metadata are misaligned. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a repeatable release workflow with validation before bundling.
actual_capabilities: Runs a patch version bump for a Blocklet project.
Detects whether package.json includes a build script.
Installs dependencies and runs the project build when required.
Finds index.html in common output directories.
Updates blocklet.yml main when it points to the wrong output directory.
Runs Blocklet metadata validation and creates a release bundle.
limitations: Requires the Blocklet CLI and pnpm to be available when commands run.
Uses a patch version bump workflow and does not describe major or minor releases.
Runs project and dependency scripts, so it should be used only in trusted repositories.
Stops when blocklet.yml is missing instead of converting the project.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Routine Patch Release
use_cases.description: Bump the Blocklet version, build the app, validate metadata, and create a release bundle.

use_cases.title: Fix Release Output Alignment
use_cases.description: Find the generated index.html and update blocklet.yml when the main field points to the wrong directory.

use_cases.title: Validate a Static Blocklet
use_cases.description: Skip build steps for a static project, check the root entry point, and bundle after metadata validation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Blocklet Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new patch release for this Blocklet project. Stop if any validation or build step fails.

prompt_templates.title: Build and Bundle
prompt_templates.prompt: Bump the Blocklet version, install dependencies, run the build, verify metadata, and create the release bundle.

prompt_templates.title: Repair Main Field
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Blocklet release and check whether blocklet.yml main matches the directory that contains index.html.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full Blocklet release workflow, explain any failure with the exact command that failed, and do not bundle until all checks pass.

output_examples.output: The version is bumped, dependencies are installed, the build succeeds, metadata validates, and the release bundle is created.

output_examples.input: Create a release, but the build output moved to build.
output_examples.output: The workflow finds the entry point in the build directory, updates the main field, validates metadata, and creates the bundle.
The workflow skips the build, confirms the root entry point, validates Blocklet metadata, and creates the release bundle.

best_practices: Run this skill in a clean working tree before starting a release.
Review package scripts and dependency changes before allowing install or build commands.
Check the generated bundle and blocklet.yml changes before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow in an untrusted repository.
Do not bundle after a failed build, metadata check, or missing entry point.
Do not use it to convert non-Blocklet projects into Blocklets.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It automates patch version bumping, optional build steps, entry point checks, metadata validation, and release bundle creation.

faq.question: Does it modify project files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can bump the project version and update the blocklet.yml main field when the output directory is misaligned.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The workflow runs external commands, including dependency installation and project build scripts, which can execute code from the repository.

faq.question: Can it handle projects without a build script?
faq.answer: Yes. It skips dependency installation and build steps when no build script is present.

faq.question: What happens when index.html is missing?
faq.answer: The workflow stops and reports that no entry point was found in the expected output directories.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
